intro: Grumilea aurantiaca is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the golden Grumilea or orange Grumilea. Traditionally, Grumilea aurantiaca has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Grumilea aurantiaca include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Grumilea aurantiaca is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis, as well as to reduce fever and promote relaxation.
